expectations.text: States that Premium Bond prizes are tax-free
expectations.evidence: Line 20 states 'your Premium Bond winnings are completely tax-free' and line 69 states 'entirely exempt from Income Tax'
Lines 10-14 show all three sources (Marcus £870, Nationwide £320, NS&I £410) totaling £1,600, confirmed again in lines 54-57

expectations.text: Applies the PSA correctly (£1,000 for basic rate taxpayer at £48,000)
expectations.evidence: Lines 40-46 present PSA table, identify employment income £48,000 as basic rate taxpayer, and correctly apply £1,000 PSA

expectations.text: Calculates tax due on £600 excess interest above PSA
expectations.evidence: Line 61 states 'you would owe £120 in tax on that excess £600', correctly calculating 20% of £600

expectations.text: Explains that banks pay interest gross since 2016 (no automatic tax deduction)
expectations.evidence: Lines 26-27 state 'banks now pay interest gross (without deducting tax at source) — this changed back in April 2016'

expectations.text: Explains how HMRC collects the tax (tax code adjustment or Self Assessment)
expectations.evidence: Lines 28-32 explain tax code adjustment mechanism, and lines 73-82 discuss Self Assessment registration threshold and notification methods as alternative collection mechanisms
